page: OVHcloud c2-30 is a compute-optimized virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, running at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. The processor is based on the Intel Haswell architecture without TSX and includes 16 MB of L3 cache. The hardware configuration is bundled with 30.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.75 GB of memory per core, alongside a 200 GB local SSD and one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. However, multi-core performance is average, as demonstrated by a stress-ng multi-core score of 6708.13 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 4585.0. Memory read bandwidth is average at 57.3 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, though large block memory write bandwidth is weak at 23.36 GB/sec. The server handles multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and software compilation with clang at average speeds, while single-threaded compression tasks remain weak.

In terms of workload suitability, the c2-30 is configured for multi-threaded applications, static web serving, and moderate database operations, as reflected by its average Passmark database score and Redis SET performance of 10.12 million operations per second. It can handle light LLM inference tasks on small models, but performance drops significantly on medium models. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated physical cores offer predictable multi-threaded performance without hyperthreading overhead, making it a stable choice for parallel processing workloads that do not require modern AVX-512 instruction sets.
description: The OVHcloud c2-30 is an x86_64 compute-optimized virtual server featuring 8 dedicated physical Intel cores running at 3.0 GHz with no hyperthreading. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, it is equipped with 30.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 200 GB local SSD. Benchmark data indicates that while single-core performance is weak, the server delivers average multi-core performance, as demonstrated by its Geekbench multi-core score of 4585.0 and stress-ng multi-core score of 6708.13. Memory read bandwidth is average at 57.3 GB/sec, though memory write bandwidth is weak. The server is suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, and database operations, providing predictable dedicated CPU resources for parallel tasks without hyperthreading overhead.
og_description: OVHcloud c2-30 is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 8 dedicated Intel cores at 3.0 GHz, 30 GB of RAM, and a 200 GB SSD, delivering reliable multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: OVHcloud c2-30 is a KVM-based compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated Intel cores, 30 GB RAM, and a 200 GB SSD for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core virtual server offering balanced multi-threaded performance and local SSD storage for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated physical Intel cores running at 3.0 GHz without hyperthreading
30.0 GB of system memory and a bundled 200 GB local SSD
Average multi-core CPU and software compilation performance
Weak single-core processing and memory write speeds
Suitable for static web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded tasks
